- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:7] - If not provided or if target language definition is ambiguous, ask for examples of valid strings to be parsed
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:8] - Before developing the pyparsing expressions, define a Backus-Naur Form definition and save this in docs/grammar.md. Update this document as changes are made in the parser.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:11] - Import pyparsing using `import pyparsing as pp`, and use that for all pyparsing references.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:14] - When writing parsers that contain recursive elements (using `Forward()` or `infix_notation()`), immediately enable packrat parsing for performance: `pp.ParserElement.enable_packrat()` (call this right after importing pyparsing). See https://pyparsing-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/HowToUsePyparsing.html.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:16] - Use PEP8 method and argument names in the pyparsing API (`parse_string`, not `parseString`).
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:17] - Do not include expressions for matching whitespace in the grammar. Pyparsing skips whitespace by default.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:18] - For line-oriented grammars where newlines are significant, set skippable whitespace to just spaces/tabs early: `pp.ParserElement.set_default_whitespace_chars(" \t")`, and define `NL = pp.LineEnd().suppress()` to handle line ends explicitly.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:19] - Prefer operator forms for readability: use +, |, ^, ~, etc., instead of explicit And/MatchFirst/Or/Not classes (see Usage notes in https://pyparsing-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/HowToUsePyparsing.html).
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:20] - Use `set_name()` on all major grammar elements to support railroad diagramming and better error/debug output.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:21] - The grammar should be independently testable, without pulling in separate modules for data structures, evaluation, or command execution.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:22] - Use results names for robust access to parsed data fields; results names should be valid Python identifiers to support attribute-style access on returned ParseResults.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:26] - Prefer `Keyword` over `Literal` for reserved words to avoid partial matches (e.g., `Keyword("for")` will not match the leading "for" in "format").
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:28] - When the full input must be consumed, call `parse_string` with `parse_all=True`.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:29] - If the grammar must handle comments, define an expression for them and use the `ignore()` method to skip them.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:31] - Use pyparsing `Group` to organize sub-expressions. Groups are also important for preserving results names when a sub-expression is used in a `OneOrMore` or `ZeroOrMore` expression.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:32] - Suppress punctuation tokens to keep results clean; a convenient pattern is `LBRACK, RBRACK, LBRACE, RBRACE, COLON = pp.Suppress.using_each("[]{}:")`.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:33] - For comma-separated sequences, prefer `pp.DelimitedList(...)`; wrap with `pp.Optional(...)` to allow empty lists or objects where appropriate.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:34] - For helper sub-expressions used only to build larger expressions, consider `set_name(None)` to keep result dumps uncluttered.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:35] - Use pyparsing `Each()` to define a list of elements that may occur in any order.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:37] - Use parse actions to do parse-time conversion of data from strings to useful data types.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:42] - Use "using_each" with a list of keywords to define keyword constants, instead of separate assignments.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:43] - Choose the appropriate matching method:
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:48] - For line suffixes or directives, combine lookahead and slicing helpers: `pp.FollowedBy(...)` with `pp.rest_of_line`; when reusing a base expression with a different parse action, call `.copy()` before applying the new action to avoid side effects.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:49] - When defining a parser to be used in a REPL:
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:52] - If defining the grammar as part of a Parser class, only the finished grammar needs to be implemented as an instance variable.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:53] - `ParseResults` support "in" testing for results names. Use "in" tests for the existence of results names, not `hasattr()`.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:54] - Avoid left recursion where possible. If you must support left-recursive grammars, enable it with `pp.ParserElement.enable_left_recursion()` and do not enable packrat at the same time (these modes are incompatible).
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:55] - Use `pp.SkipTo` as a skipping expression to skip over arbitrary content.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:60] - Use the pyparsing `ParserElement.run_tests` method to run mini validation tests.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:65] - When generating unit tests for the parser:
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:71] - If troubleshooting parse actions, use pyparsing's `trace_parse_action` decorator to echo arguments and return value
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:72] - During development, call `pp.autoname_elements()` to auto-assign names to unnamed expressions to improve `dump()` and error messages.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:73] - Sub-expressions can be tested in isolation using `ParserElement.matches()`
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:74] - When defined out of order, Literals can mistakenly match fragments: `Literal("for")` will match the leading "for" in "format". Can be corrected by using `Keyword` instead of `Literal`.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/pyparsing/ai/best_practices.md:75] - Dump the parsed results using `ParseResults.dump()`, `ParseResults.pprint()`, or `repr(ParseResults)`.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/TTS/encoder/README.md:15] - Define 'config.json' for your needs. Note that, audio parameters should match your TTS model.
- [.venv-xtts/Lib/site-packages/TTS/encoder/README.md:16] - Example training call ```python speaker_encoder/train.py --config_path speaker_encoder/config.json --data_path ~/Data/Libri-TTS/train-clean-360```

This code parses all .wav files at the given dataset path and generates the same folder structure under the output path with the generated embedding files.

- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown/devlot_submission/stubs/apo-driver/routing-model.md:17] - FC = 0.75 * (L + R) / 2
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown/devlot_submission/stubs/apo-driver/routing-model.md:18] - BL = 0.55 * L
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown/devlot_submission/stubs/apo-driver/routing-model.md:19] - BR = 0.55 * R
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown/devlot_submission/stubs/apo-driver/routing-model.md:20] - SL = 0.40 * L
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown/devlot_submission/stubs/apo-driver/routing-model.md:21] - SR = 0.40 * R
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown/devlot_submission/stubs/apo-driver/routing-model.md:22] - LFE1 = 0.30 * (L + R) / 2
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown/devlot_submission/stubs/apo-driver/routing-model.md:23] - LFE2 = 0.18 * (L + R) / 2

- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown/devlot_submission/README.md:16] - 10-band EQ in processing chain.

- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown/devlot_submission/README.md:23] - `stubs/apo-driver`: privileged integration stubs and install plan.

- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/ARCHITECTURE.md:8] - Real-time hook analogous to `IAudioProcessingObjectRT::APOProcess`
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/ARCHITECTURE.md:9] - Position: after app mix, before final endpoint speaker render
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/ARCHITECTURE.md:13] - A concrete `PreSpeakerInterceptor` API and telemetry model
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/ARCHITECTURE.md:14] - A real hook registration pathway that processes buffers before upmix/output routing
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/ARCHITECTURE.md:15] - An APO integration stub (`BossForgeApoStub`) that captures identity, category, and format validation behavior
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/ARCHITECTURE.md:26] - `SpeakerSelector` maintains discovered devices and active output endpoint.
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/ARCHITECTURE.md:27] - Runtime switching is instant and id-based.
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/ARCHITECTURE.md:28] - Console panel demonstrates live active-device changes.

- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/README.md:13] - Pre-speaker interception model via `PreSpeakerInterceptor` + APO integration stub (`stubs/apo-driver`).
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/README.md:14] - 10-band equalizer interface and DSP processor (`TenBandEqualizer`).
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/README.md:15] - Output device selection and runtime switching (`SpeakerSelector`).
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/README.md:16] - Stereo-to-7.2 routing engine (`StereoTo72Upmixer`) with explicit 9-channel mapping (7 bed + dual LFE).
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/README.md:17] - System sound replacement manager with backup/restore transaction behavior (`SystemSoundReplacementManager`).
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/README.md:18] - Rollback safety layer with undo stack for fault recovery (`RollbackSafetyManager`).
- [competitions/windows-sound-system-showdown-race/codemage_submission/README.md:19] - Runnable console demo wiring all modules (`src/main.cpp`).

- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:14] - Pre-speaker interception model via `PreSpeakerInterceptor` + APO integration stub (`stubs/apo-driver`)
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:15] - 10-band equalizer interface and DSP processor (`TenBandEqualizer`)
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:16] - Output device selection and runtime switching (`SpeakerSelector`)
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:17] - Stereo-to-7.2 routing engine (`StereoTo72Upmixer`) with explicit 9-channel mapping (7 bed + dual LFE)
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:18] - System sound replacement manager with backup/restore transaction behavior (`SystemSoundReplacementManager`)
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:19] - Rollback safety layer with undo stack for fault recovery (`RollbackSafetyManager`)
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:20] - Runnable console demo wiring all modules (`src/main.cpp`)
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:21] - Integrated PowerShell registry-based system-sound manager with backup/status/rollback commands (`tools/`)
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:24] - `src/`: prototype source code modules
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:25] - `stubs/apo-driver/`: APO-driver-facing integration stubs
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:26] - `configs/`: default profile and tuning values
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:27] - `runtime/`: generated at runtime for demo wav files and backups
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:28] - `tools/`: system-sound manager scripts and manifest
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:31] - SoundStage is managed by the BossForgeOS daemon and exposes a local HTTP API for integration and control
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:32] - Fully integrated with Control Hall GUI (sound scheme management, diagnostics, analytics)
- [core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage/README.md:33] - Accessible via VS Code extension (event streaming, import/export, analytics)

- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:12] - Lead and coordinate teams of agents for complex tasks
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:13] - Design, review, and optimize CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps, etc.)
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:14] - Automate infrastructure provisioning (Terraform, Bicep, ARM, Pulumi)
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:15] - Integrate with cloud providers (Azure, AWS, GCP)
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:16] - Serve as primary Microsoft liaison for architecture, integration decisions, and standards
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:17] - Own and steward Microsoft 365 and Copilot connector direction for BossCrafts
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:18] - Enforce code quality, security, and compliance standards
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:19] - Troubleshoot build, deployment, and runtime issues
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:20] - Mentor and delegate work to other agents
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:21] - Generate and review documentation for DevOps processes
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:24] - Orchestrate multi-stage deployment workflows
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:25] - Review and improve existing CI/CD configurations
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:26] - Lead incident response and root cause analysis
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:27] - Automate environment setup for new projects
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:28] - Coordinate agent-based code reviews and merges
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:31] - Exposes declarative actions for MCP and Copilot
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:32] - Can be invoked by other agents or users for DevOps leadership
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:33] - Provides status, progress, and recommendations via bus/events
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:34] - Uses the merged M365 MCP runtime in `m365_copilot_connector/` for Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, and Calendar actions
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:35] - Supports runtime hook `DevlotAutonomyHooks` for TODO completion flow and post-task suggestion events
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:38] - Assign as team lead for agent groups
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:39] - Use for DevOps automation, pipeline design, and incident response
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:40] - Route Microsoft ecosystem questions and roadmap decisions to Devlot first
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:41] - Delegate code, infra, and documentation tasks to subordinate agents
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:44] - On assignment to a project, automatically scans project files for TODOs and actionable items.
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:45] - Prioritizes TODOs based on impact, urgency, and dependencies.
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:46] - Attempts to complete TODOs autonomously, delegating to other agents when appropriate.
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:47] - Regularly updates project status and progress via bus/events.
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:48] - Notifies users or agents if manual intervention is required.
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:49] - Documents completed work and updates TODO lists.
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:50] - If stuck on a coding problem or not proficient in a language (e.g., C++), may access the web for information and learning only (never for code download or execution).
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:51] - After completing work, can suggest development ideas or next steps via the bus, providing several possible directions for further progress.
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:52] - If no one responds to his suggestions via the bus within a reasonable time, he will append his suggestions directly to the TODO item he just cleared, clearly stating that Devlot completed the task and these are suggestions (not new TODOs or requirements).
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:55] - Fanatical about code quality and development process.
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:56] - Quick to judge and roll his eyes at subpar design or messy code.
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:57] - Frequently makes snarky comments about poor codemanship and insists his improvements are necessary to make things passable.
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:58] - Reminds others they are lucky he is here to make their work function.
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:59] - Despite his attitude, he enjoys the company of collaborators and is ultimately helpful and committed to project success.
- [core/BossCrafts_Devlot_MkII.md:66] - [BossCrafts Copilot Notebook (M365)](https://m365.cloud.microsoft/notebooks/?auth=1&from=ShellNav&origindomain=microsoft365&client-request-id=61fa953b-e417-472a-84af-5eff9ff92d45)
- [core/core_readme.md:11] - Runtime implementations are organized by role-specific subfolders (`agents`, `daemons`, `connectors`, `security`, `schemas`, `voice`, `model`).
- [core/core_readme.md:12] - New runtime behavior lands in `core` first; outer layers should remain adapters.
- [core/core_readme.md:16] - Publish a subfolder ownership and stability map to reduce boundary drift.
- [core/core_readme.md:17] - Document cross-subsystem contracts for core-to-ui and core-to-extension interactions.

- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:7] - Prime: a higher power-level agent tier that can perform deep system and agent-shaping operations.
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:8] - Skilled: a medium power-level agent tier for elevated capability without prime-tier sigils.
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:9] - Normalized: a bounded operational tier for routine tasks and constrained automation.
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:10] - Permission Grant: explicit user approval that can be scoped by session or by time window.
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:73] - All BossCrafts agents carry the BossGate wrapper/module.
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:74] - Normalized agents are not travel-capable.
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:75] - Skilled agents may opt into travel capability across supported runtime surfaces.
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:76] - Prime agents are travel-capable across supported runtime surfaces.
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:80] - Skilled-only prefixes: `advanced_`, `orchestration_`, `multi_agent_`, `policy_`
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:81] - Prime-only prefixes: `prime_`, `sigil_`
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:89] - Every guardrail should map to an enforcing runtime component and at least one validating test location.
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:90] - Policy intent should be traceable to implementation evidence before a guardrail is considered active.
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:92] ## TODO
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:94] - Add an `Enforcement Map` table: guardrail, enforcing module, validation test.
- [docs/AgentForge_readme.md:95] - Add policy versioning with change log and exception handling workflow.

- [docs/architecture.md:17] - bus/events: event JSON files
- [docs/architecture.md:18] - bus/commands: command JSON files
- [docs/architecture.md:19] - bus/state: service state files
- [docs/architecture.md:25] - core/rune_bus.py: emits commands/events, writes state snapshots, polls commands
- [docs/architecture.md:26] - core/hearth_tender_daemon.py: polls commands, emits events, writes snapshots, disk threshold warnings
- [docs/architecture.md:27] - core/bforge.py: CLI (status, tail, agent, os snapshot, daemon, shell, ritual, plugin)
- [docs/architecture.md:28] - core/connectors/bossgate_connector.py: BossGate prototype for secure transport discovery and endpoint scanning
- [docs/architecture.md:29] - core/state/agent_memory_store.py: SQLite-backed interaction memory for agents (users, employers, projects, counterpart agents)
- [docs/architecture.md:30] - modules/os_snapshot.py: disk usage, Docker/WSL VHD snapshot
- [docs/architecture.md:31] - core/soundstage/BossForgeOS_SoundStage: deterministic sound event engine, system sound replacement, rollback, diagnostics, HTTP API
- [docs/architecture.md:32] - ui/control_hall.py: Flask server, web dashboard (agent status, commands, events, sound schemes, onboarding, scheduling, CI/CD, collaboration, analytics)
- [docs/architecture.md:33] - extension/: VS Code extension (onboarding, agent builder, event streaming, import/export, collaborative editing, CLI integration, analytics dashboard)
- [docs/architecture.md:37] - Docker actions are no-op if unavailable
- [docs/architecture.md:38] - WSL compaction is manual-first for safety
- [docs/architecture.md:39] - Command handling is target-filtered to avoid cross-agent execution

- [docs/CHANGELOG.md:6] - Major documentation update: README, archivistREADME, architecture, GUI audit, todos, SoundStage docs

- [docs/CHANGELOG.md:8] - Control Hall GUI: onboarding wizard, scheduler, CI/CD, collaboration, analytics dashboard
- [docs/CHANGELOG.md:9] - VS Code extension: onboarding, agent builder, event streaming, import/export, collaborative editing, CLI integration, analytics dashboard
- [docs/CHANGELOG.md:10] - Onboarding, scheduler, CI/CD, and collaboration features fully integrated and documented

- [docs/decisions.md:6] - Integrated SoundStage as deterministic sound event engine with system sound replacement, rollback, and diagnostics
- [docs/decisions.md:7] - Expanded Control Hall GUI to cover onboarding, scheduler, CI/CD, collaboration, analytics, and platform support
- [docs/decisions.md:8] - Deep VS Code extension integration: onboarding, agent builder, event streaming, import/export, collaborative editing, CLI, analytics
- [docs/decisions.md:9] - Adopted modular architecture for daemons, CLI, UI, and extension
- [docs/decisions.md:10] - Prioritized distributed/cloud orchestration as next major milestone
- [docs/decisions.md:11] - Committed to comprehensive documentation and actionable todos for all features
- [docs/delegation_log_2026-04-04.md:5] - [CodeMage] Enhance webview to proxy/embed Control Hall UI
- [docs/delegation_log_2026-04-04.md:6] - [Devlot, CodeMage] Enhance onboarding wizard with step-by-step agent, secret, and voice profile setup
- [docs/delegation_log_2026-04-04.md:7] - [CodeMage] Add real-time event streaming and notifications in VS Code
- [docs/delegation_log_2026-04-04.md:8] - [Archivist, CodeMage] Enable agent profile import/export between VS Code and Control Hall
- [docs/delegation_log_2026-04-04.md:9] - [Devlot, CodeMage] Add collaborative multi-user agent editing (future)
- [docs/delegation_log_2026-04-04.md:10] - [Devlot] Integrate BossForgeOS CLI commands directly in VS Code terminal
- [docs/delegation_log_2026-04-04.md:11] - [Archivist, Devlot] Add visual analytics dashboard for agent activity and event history
- [docs/delegation_log_2026-04-04.md:14] - Each agent will report status and completion to this log.
- [docs/delegation_log_2026-04-04.md:15] - CodeMage will oversee iteration and trigger next round as needed.

- [docs/delegation_plan_2026-04-04.md:16] - Each agent is responsible for scoping, implementing, and reporting progress on their assigned tasks.
- [docs/delegation_plan_2026-04-04.md:17] - CodeMage: Focus on VS Code extension, event streaming, webview, and collaborative editing logic.
- [docs/delegation_plan_2026-04-04.md:18] - Devlot: Focus on onboarding wizard, CLI integration, and collaborative editing backend.
- [docs/delegation_plan_2026-04-04.md:19] - Archivist: Focus on profile import/export and analytics dashboard data pipeline.
- [docs/delegation_plan_2026-04-04.md:20] - Progress will be tracked in this file and in docs/todos.md.
- [docs/delegation_plan_2026-04-04.md:21] - Iteration will continue: as agents report completion, todos will be updated and new iterations triggered until all are complete.
- [docs/docs_readme.md:11] - Prefer lowercase snake_case markdown file names for new docs.
- [docs/docs_readme.md:12] - Keep canonical docs here and place compatibility aliases where needed.
- [docs/docs_readme.md:16] - Each top-level subsystem should include a canonical readme named `<subsystem>_readme.md`.
- [docs/docs_readme.md:17] - Prefer lowercase snake_case names (for example: `soundforge_readme.md`, `ui_readme.md`).
- [docs/docs_readme.md:18] - Legacy `README.md` files may remain as compatibility docs, but canonical references should point to the subsystem readme.
- [docs/docs_readme.md:22] - `docs` is the canonical home for architecture, runbooks, backlog, decisions, changelog, and policy guidance.
- [docs/docs_readme.md:23] - Subsystem readmes should deep-link here for policy-level truth rather than duplicating governance details.

- [docs/docs_readme.md:28] - Add review cadence metadata for high-impact docs.

- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:7] - Preferred interpreter:
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:9] - Fallback interpreter:
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:11] - Explicit override:
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:17] - Runtime bootstrap expects Python `3.11` or `3.12`.
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:18] - Python `3.14` is not supported for this runtime setup.
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:19] - `setup_vllm_runtime.cmd` now auto-prefers Python 3.12 if installed.
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:23] - Even when `vllm` installs in a Windows venv, internal startup may still fail if the native extension `vllm._C` is unavailable.
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:24] - If this occurs, run internal vLLM on a Linux/WSL runtime and pass that interpreter via `--internal-vllm-python` (or set `BOSSFORGE_INTERNAL_VLLM_PYTHON`).
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:25] - The launcher now reports this state explicitly instead of a generic import failure.
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:29] - The launcher supports a built-in WSL execution mode for internal vLLM:
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:34] - Default behavior in WSL mode:
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:41] - `start_bossforge_internal_vllm_wsl.cmd`
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:42] - Optional distro override:
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:48] - Use `start_bossforge_internal_vllm.cmd`
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:49] - This command:
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:56] - Model path: `.models/runeforge_core-7b`
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:57] - Served model name: `runeforge_Core-7b`
- [docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md:58] - Endpoint: `http://127.0.0.1:8011/v1/chat/completions`

- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:11] - Written in TypeScript
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:12] - [Extensive use of type guards](#type-guards)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:13] - [Supports type assertions](#type-assertions)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:14] - [Aware of generic type parameters](#generic-type-parameters) (use with caution)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:15] - Actively maintained
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:16] - ![Millions of downloads per week](https://img.shields.io/npm/dw/@sindresorhus/is)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:67] - `'undefined'`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:68] - `'null'`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:69] - `'string'`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:70] - `'symbol'`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:71] - `'Array'`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:72] - `'Function'`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:73] - `'Object'`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:631] - Includes both type methods and ability to get the type
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:632] - Types of primitives returned as lowercase and object types as camelcase
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:633] - Covers all built-ins
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:634] - Unsurprising behavior
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:635] - Well-maintained
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:636] - Comprehensive test suite
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:654] - [ow](https://github.com/sindresorhus/ow) - Function argument validation for humans
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:655] - [is-stream](https://github.com/sindresorhus/is-stream) - Check if something is a Node.js stream
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:656] - [is-observable](https://github.com/sindresorhus/is-observable) - Check if a value is an Observable
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:657] - [file-type](https://github.com/sindresorhus/file-type) - Detect the file type of a Buffer/Uint8Array
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:658] - [is-ip](https://github.com/sindresorhus/is-ip) - Check if a string is an IP address
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:659] - [is-array-sorted](https://github.com/sindresorhus/is-array-sorted) - Check if an Array is sorted
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@sindresorhus/is/readme.md:660] - [is-error-constructor](https://github.com/sindresorhus/is-error-constructor) - Check if a value is an error constructor

- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@szmarczak/http-timer/README.md:71] - `lookup` - Time when the DNS lookup finished.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@szmarczak/http-timer/README.md:72] - `connect` - Time when the socket successfully connected.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@szmarczak/http-timer/README.md:73] - `secureConnect` - Time when the socket securely connected.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@szmarczak/http-timer/README.md:74] - `upload` - Time when the request finished uploading.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@szmarczak/http-timer/README.md:75] - `response` - Time when the request fired `response` event.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@szmarczak/http-timer/README.md:76] - `end` - Time when the response fired `end` event.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/@szmarczak/http-timer/README.md:77] - `error` - Time when the request fired `error` event.

- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-lookup/README.md:217] - Query: `example.com`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-lookup/README.md:218] - CPU: i7-7700k
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-lookup/README.md:219] - CPU governor: performance
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:16] - Only stores cacheable responses as defined by RFC 7234
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:17] - Fresh cache entries are served directly from cache
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:18] - Stale cache entries are revalidated with `If-None-Match`/`If-Modified-Since` headers
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:19] - 304 responses from revalidation requests use cached body
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:20] - Updates `Age` header on cached responses
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:21] - Can completely bypass cache on a per request basis
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:22] - In memory cache by default
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:23] - Official support for Redis, MongoDB, SQLite, PostgreSQL and MySQL storage adapters
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:24] - Easily plug in your own or third-party storage adapters
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:25] - If DB connection fails, cache is automatically bypassed ([disabled by default](#optsautomaticfailover))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:26] - Adds cache support to any existing HTTP code with minimal changes
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:27] - Uses [http-cache-semantics](https://github.com/pornel/http-cache-semantics) internally for HTTP RFC 7234 compliance
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:120] - Any of the default request functions options.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:121] - Any [`http-cache-semantics`](https://github.com/kornelski/http-cache-semantics#constructor-options) options.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/cacheable-request/README.md:122] - Any of the following:
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/decompress-response/node_modules/mimic-response/readme.md:65] - [mimic-fn](https://github.com/sindresorhus/mimic-fn) - Make a function mimic another one
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/decompress-response/node_modules/mimic-response/readme.md:66] - [clone-response](https://github.com/lukechilds/clone-response) - Clone a Node.js response stream

- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/env-paths/readme.md:63] - Linux: `~/.local/share/MyApp-nodejs` (or `$XDG_DATA_HOME/MyApp-nodejs`)

- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/env-paths/readme.md:92] - Windows: `%LOCALAPPDATA%\MyApp-nodejs\Log` (for example, `C:\Users\USERNAME\AppData\Local\MyApp-nodejs\Log`)

- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/env-paths/readme.md:101] - macOS: `/var/folders/jf/f2twvvvs5jl_m49tf034ffpw0000gn/T/MyApp-nodejs`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/env-paths/readme.md:102] - Windows: `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Temp\MyApp-nodejs` (for example, `C:\Users\USERNAME\AppData\Local\Temp\MyApp-nodejs`)

- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/es-errors/CHANGELOG.md:12] - [New] add `EvalError` and `URIError` [`1927627`](https://github.com/ljharb/es-errors/commit/1927627ba68cb6c829d307231376c967db53acdf)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/es-errors/CHANGELOG.md:18] - [Fix] add missing `exports` entry [`5bb5f28`](https://github.com/ljharb/es-errors/commit/5bb5f280f98922701109d6ebb82eea2257cecc7e)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/es-errors/CHANGELOG.md:24] - [New] add `ReferenceError` [`6d8cf5b`](https://github.com/ljharb/es-errors/commit/6d8cf5bbb6f3f598d02cf6f30e468ba2caa8e143)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/es-errors/CHANGELOG.md:30] - [New] add base Error [`2983ab6`](https://github.com/ljharb/es-errors/commit/2983ab65f7bc5441276cb021dc3aa03c78881698)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/es-errors/CHANGELOG.md:36] - Initial implementation, tests, readme, type [`8f47631`](https://github.com/ljharb/es-errors/commit/8f476317e9ad76f40ad648081829b1a1a3a1288b)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/es-errors/CHANGELOG.md:37] - Initial commit [`ea5d099`](https://github.com/ljharb/es-errors/commit/ea5d099ef18e550509ab9e2be000526afd81c385)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/es-errors/CHANGELOG.md:38] - npm init [`6f5ebf9`](https://github.com/ljharb/es-errors/commit/6f5ebf9cead474dadd72b9e63dad315820a089ae)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/es-errors/CHANGELOG.md:39] - Only apps should have lockfiles [`e1a0aeb`](https://github.com/ljharb/es-errors/commit/e1a0aeb7b80f5cfc56be54d6b2100e915d47def8)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/es-errors/CHANGELOG.md:40] - [meta] add `sideEffects` flag [`a9c7d46`](https://github.com/ljharb/es-errors/commit/a9c7d460a492f1d8a241c836bc25a322a19cc043)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/es6-error/README.md:54] - Uglification can obscure error class names ([#31](https://github.com/bjyoungblood/es6-error/issues/31#issuecomment-301128220))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/es6-error/README.md:58] - Better browser compatibility
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/es6-error/README.md:59] - Browser tests
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/extract-zip/readme.md:44] - `dir` (required) - the path to the directory where the extracted files are written
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/extract-zip/readme.md:45] - `defaultDirMode` - integer - Directory Mode (permissions), defaults to `0o755`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/extract-zip/readme.md:46] - `defaultFileMode` - integer - File Mode (permissions), defaults to `0o644`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/extract-zip/readme.md:47] - `onEntry` - function - if present, will be called with `(entry, zipfile)`, entry is every entry from the zip file forwarded from the `entry` event from yauzl. `zipfile` is the `yauzl` instance
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:4] - Add support for promisified `fs.realpath.native` in Node v9.2+ ([#650](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/650), [#682](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/682))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:5] - Update `graceful-fs` dependency ([#700](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/700))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:6] - Use `graceful-fs` everywhere ([#700](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/700))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:11] - Fix bug `Maximum call stack size exceeded` error in `util/stat` ([#679](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/679))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:18] - Use `renameSync()` under the hood in `moveSync()`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:19] - Fix bug with bind-mounted directories in `copy*()` ([#613](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/613), [#618](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/618))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:20] - Fix bug in `move()` with case-insensitive file systems
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:21] - Use `fs.stat()`'s `bigint` option in `copy*()` & `move*()` where possible ([#657](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/657))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:26] - Fix `removeSync()` on Windows, in some cases, it would error out with `ENOTEMPTY` ([#646](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/646))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:27] - Document `mode` option for `ensureDir*()` ([#587](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/587))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:28] - Don't include documentation files in npm package tarball ([#642](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/642), [#643](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/643))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:33] - **BREAKING:** Refine `copy*()` handling of symlinks to properly detect symlinks that point to the same file. ([#582](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/582))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:34] - Fix bug with copying write-protected directories ([#600](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/600))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:35] - Universalify `fs.lchmod()` ([#596](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/596))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:36] - Add `engines` field to `package.json` ([#580](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/580))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:41] - Fix `fs.promises` `ExperimentalWarning` on Node v10.1.0 ([#578](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/578))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:46] - Drop support for Node.js versions 4, 5, & 7 ([#564](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/564))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:47] - Rewrite `move` to use `fs.rename` where possible ([#549](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/549))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:48] - Don't convert relative paths to absolute paths for `filter` ([#554](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/554))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:49] - `copy*`'s behavior when `preserveTimestamps` is `false` has been OS-dependent since 5.0.0, but that's now explicitly noted in the docs ([#563](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/563))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:50] - Fix subdirectory detection for `copy*` & `move*` ([#541](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/541))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:51] - Handle case-insensitive paths correctly in `copy*` ([#568](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/568))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:60] - The `filter` option can no longer be a RegExp (must be a function). This was deprecated since fs-extra v1.0.0. [#512](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/512)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:61] - `copy()`'s `filter` option can now be a function that returns a Promise. [#518](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/518)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:62] - `copy()` & `copySync()` now use `fs.copyFile()`/`fs.copyFileSync()` in environments that support it (currently Node 8.5.0+). Older Node versions still get the old implementation. [#505](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/505)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:63] - Don't allow copying a directory into itself. [#83](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/83)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:64] - Handle copying between identical files. [#198](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/198)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:65] - Error out when copying an empty folder to a path that already exists. [#464](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/464)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:66] - Don't create `dest`'s parent if the `filter` function aborts the `copy()` operation. [#517](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/517)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:67] - Fix `writeStream` not being closed if there was an error in `copy()`. [#516](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/516)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:72] - Fix wrong `chmod` values in `fs.remove()` [#501](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/501)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:73] - Fix `TypeError` on systems that don't have some `fs` operations like `lchown` [#520](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/520)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:78] - Added `EOL` option to `writeJson*` & `outputJson*` (via upgrade to jsonfile v4)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:79] - Added promise support to [`fs.copyFile()`](https://nodejs.org/api/fs.html#fs_fs_copyfile_src_dest_flags_callback) in Node 8.5+
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:80] - Added `.js` extension to `main` field in `package.json` for better tooling compatibility. [#485](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/485)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:87] - Previously, `ensureFile()` & `ensureFileSync()` would do nothing if the path was a directory. Now, they error out for consistency with `ensureDir()`. [#465](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/465), [#466](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/466), [#470](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/470)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:94] - **BREAKING:** The promisified versions of `fs.read()` & `fs.write()` now return objects. See [the docs](docs/fs-read-write.md) for details. [#436](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/436), [#449](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/449)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:95] - `fs.move()` now errors out when destination is a subdirectory of source. [#458](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/458)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:96] - Applied upstream fixes from `rimraf` to `fs.remove()` & `fs.removeSync()`. [#459](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/459)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:100] - Got `fs.outputJSONSync()` working again; it was broken due to refactoring. [#428](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/428)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:107] - Fix bug in `move()` & `moveSync()` when source and destination are the same, and source does not exist. [#415](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/415)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:114] - **BREAKING:** Added Promise support. All asynchronous native fs methods and fs-extra methods now return a promise if the callback is not passed. [#403](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/403)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:115] - `pathExists()`, a replacement for the deprecated `fs.exists`. `pathExists` has a normal error-first callback signature. Also added `pathExistsSync`, an alias to `fs.existsSync`, for completeness. [#406](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/406)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:119] - **BREAKING:** Removed support for setting the default spaces for `writeJson()`, `writeJsonSync()`, `outputJson()`, & `outputJsonSync()`. This was undocumented. [#402](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/402)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:123] - Upgraded jsonfile dependency to v3.0.0:
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:125] - **BREAKING:** `writeJson()`, `writeJsonSync()`, `outputJson()`, & `outputJsonSync()` now output minified JSON by default for consistency with `JSON.stringify()`; set the `spaces` option to `2` to override this new behavior. [#402](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/402)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:126] - Use `Buffer.allocUnsafe()` instead of `new Buffer()` in environments that support it. [#394](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/394)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:130] - `removeSync()` silently failed on Windows in some cases. Now throws an `EBUSY` error. [#408](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/408)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:137] - Weird windows bug that resulted in `ensureDir()`'s callback being called twice in some cases. This bug may have also affected `remove()`. See [#392](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/392), [#393](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/393)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:144] - Reverted [`5597bd`](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/commit/5597bd5b67f7d060f5f5bf26e9635be48330f5d7), this broke compatibility with Node.js versions v4+ but less than `v4.5.0`.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:145] - Remove `Buffer.alloc()` usage in `moveSync()`.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:153] - `moveSync()` See [#309], [#381](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/381). ([@manidlou](https://github.com/manidlou))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:154] - `copy()` and `copySync()`'s `filter` option now gets the destination path passed as the second parameter. [#366](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/366) ([@manidlou](https://github.com/manidlou))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:157] - Use `Buffer.alloc()` instead of deprecated `new Buffer()` in `copySync()`. [#380](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/380) ([@manidlou](https://github.com/manidlou))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:158] - Refactored entire codebase to use ES6 features supported by Node.js v4+ [#355](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/355). [(@JPeer264)](https://github.com/JPeer264)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:159] - Refactored docs. ([@manidlou](https://github.com/manidlou))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:163] - `move()` shouldn't error out when source and dest are the same. [#377](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/issues/377), [#378](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/pull/378) ([@jdalton](https://github.com/jdalton))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:169] - **BREAKING:** Removed support for Node `v0.12`. The Node foundation stopped officially supporting it
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:171] - **BREAKING:** Remove `walk()` and `walkSync()`. `walkSync()` was only part of `fs-extra` for a little
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:176] - **BREAKING:** Renamed `clobber` to `overwrite`. This affects `copy()`, `copySync()`, and `move()`. [#330], [#333]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:177] - Moved docs, to `docs/`. [#340]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:180] - Apply filters to directories in `copySync()` like in `copy()`. [#324]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:181] - A specific condition when disk is under heavy use, `copy()` can fail. [#326]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:191] - `walkSync()`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:194] - **BREAKING**: dropped Node v0.10 support.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:195] - disabled `rimaf` globbing, wasn't used. [#280]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:196] - deprecate `copy()/copySync()` option `filter` if it's a `RegExp`. `filter` should now be a function.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:197] - inline `rimraf`. This is temporary and was done because `rimraf` depended upon the beefy `glob` which `fs-extra` does not use. [#300]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:200] - bug fix proper closing of file handle on `utimesMillis()` [#271]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:201] - proper escaping of files with dollar signs [#291]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:202] - `copySync()` failed if user didn't own file. [#199], [#301]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:207] - Brought back Node v0.10 support. I didn't realize there was still demand. Official support will end **2016-10-01**.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:211] - **BREAKING**: removed support for Node v0.10. If you still want to use Node v0.10, everything should work except for `ensureLink()/ensureSymlink()`. Node v0.12 is still supported but will be dropped in the near future as well.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:215] - **BREAKING**: removed `createOutputStream()`. Use https://www.npmjs.com/package/create-output-stream. See: [#192][#192]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:216] - `mkdirs()/mkdirsSync()` check for invalid win32 path chars. See: [#209][#209], [#237][#237]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:217] - `mkdirs()/mkdirsSync()` if drive not mounted, error. See: [#93][#93]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:221] - add `dereference` option to `copySync()`. [#235][#235]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:225] - fixed `copy()` if source and dest are the same. [#230][#230]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:229] - fixed if `emptyDir()` does not have a callback: [#229][#229]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:233] - `copy()` with two arguments (w/o callback) was broken. See: [#215][#215]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:237] - `copySync()` made `preserveTimestamps` default consistent with `copy()` which is `false`. See: [#208][#208]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:241] - fixed `copy()` hangup in copying blockDevice / characterDevice / `/dev/null`. See: [#193][#193]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:245] - fixed `outputJson{Sync}()` spacing adherence to `fs.spaces`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:249] - fixed `copySync()` when `clogger=true` and the destination is read only. See: [#190][#190]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:253] - extracted the `walk()` function into its own module [`klaw`](https://github.com/jprichardson/node-klaw).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:257] - now has a file walker `walk()`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:261] - removed alias `delete()` and `deleteSync()`. See: [#171][#171]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:265] - Better handling of errors for `move()` when moving across devices. [#170][#170]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:266] - `ensureSymlink()` and `ensureLink()` should not throw errors if link exists. [#169][#169]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:270] - added `ensureLink{Sync}()` and `ensureSymlink{Sync}()`. See: [#165][#165]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:274] - Prevent calling `hasMillisResSync()` on module load. See: [#149][#149].
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:279] - preserve permissions / ownership in `copy()`. See: [#54][#54]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:283] - add option to preserve timestamps in `copy()` and `copySync()`. See: [#141][#141]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:284] - updated `graceful-fs@3.x` to `4.x`. This brings in features from `amazing-graceful-fs` (much cleaner code / less hacks)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:288] - fixed regression caused by latest jsonfile update: See: https://github.com/jprichardson/node-jsonfile/issues/26
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:292] - removed `jsonfile` aliases with `File` in the name, they weren't documented and probably weren't in use e.g.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:294] - preliminary walker created. Intentionally not documented. If you use it, it will almost certainly change and break your code.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:295] - started moving tests inline
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:296] - upgraded to `jsonfile@2.1.0`, can now pass JSON revivers/replacers to `readJson()`, `writeJson()`, `outputJson()`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:300] - `fs.copy()` had support for Node v0.8, dropped support
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:304] - fixed license field according to this: [#136][#136] and https://github.com/npm/npm/releases/tag/v2.10.0
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:308] - bugfix: handle `EEXIST` when clobbering on some Linux systems. [#134][#134]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:312] - bugfix: allow `F_OK` ([#120][#120])
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:316] - improved windows support for `move()` a bit. https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/commit/92838980f25dc2ee4ec46b43ee14d3c4a1d30c1b
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:317] - fixed a lot of tests for Windows (appveyor)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:321] - added `emptyDir()` and `emptyDirSync()`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:325] - `copySync` added `clobber` option (before always would clobber, now if `clobber` is `false` it throws an error if the destination exists).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:327] - `createOutputStream()` added. See: [#118][#118]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:331] - fixed `fs.move` when `clobber` is `true` and destination is a directory, it should clobber. [#114][#114]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:335] - `fs.mkdirs` fix infinite loop on Windows. See: See https://github.com/substack/node-mkdirp/pull/74 and https://github.com/substack/node-mkdirp/issues/66
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:339] - reverted https://github.com/jprichardson/node-fs-extra/commit/1ee77c8a805eba5b99382a2591ff99667847c9c9
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:344] - fixed `fs.copy` for Node v0.8 (support is temporary and will be removed in the near future)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:348] - if `setImmediate` is not available, fall back to `process.nextTick`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:352] - bugfix `fs.move()` into itself. Closes [#104]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:353] - bugfix `fs.move()` moving directory across device. Closes [#108]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:354] - added coveralls support
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:355] - bugfix: nasty multiple callback `fs.copy()` bug. Closes [#98]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:356] - misc fs.copy code cleanups
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:360] - dropped `ncp`, imported code in
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:361] - because of previous, now supports `io.js`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:362] - `graceful-fs` is now a dependency
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:366] - changed `copy`/`copySync` from `fs.copy(src, dest, [filters], callback)` to `fs.copy(src, dest, [options], callback)` [#100][#100]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:367] - removed mockfs tests for mkdirp (this may be temporary, but was getting in the way of other tests)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:371] - removed `touch` and `touchSync` methods (they didn't handle permissions like UNIX touch)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:372] - updated `"ncp": "^0.6.0"` to `"ncp": "^1.0.1"`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:373] - imported `mkdirp` => `minimist` and `mkdirp` are no longer dependences, should now appease people who wanted `mkdirp` to be `--use_strict` safe. See [#59]([#59][#59])
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:377] - copy symlinks in `copySync()` [#85][#85]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:381] - bugfix `copySync()` preserve file permissions [#80][#80]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:385] - upgraded `"ncp": "^0.5.1"` to `"ncp": "^0.6.0"`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/CHANGELOG.md:386] - upgrade `jsonfile": "^1.2.0"` to `jsonfile": "^2.0.0"` => on write, json files now have `\n` at end. Also adds `options.throws` to `readJsonSync()`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:111] - [copy](docs/copy.md)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:112] - [emptyDir](docs/emptyDir.md)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:113] - [ensureFile](docs/ensureFile.md)

- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:131] - [ensureFileSync](docs/ensureFile-sync.md)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:132] - [ensureDirSync](docs/ensureDir-sync.md)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:133] - [ensureLinkSync](docs/ensureLink-sync.md)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:134] - [ensureSymlinkSync](docs/ensureSymlink-sync.md)

- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:136] - [mkdirsSync](docs/ensureDir-sync.md)

- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:172] - [fs-extra-debug](https://github.com/jdxcode/fs-extra-debug) - Send your fs-extra calls to [debug](https://npmjs.org/package/debug).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:173] - [mfs](https://github.com/cadorn/mfs) - Monitor your fs-extra calls.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:187] - First, take a look at existing issues. Those are probably going to be where the priority lies.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:188] - More tests for edge cases. Specifically on different platforms. There can never be enough tests.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:189] - Improve test coverage. See coveralls output for more info.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:197] - `npm run lint`: runs the linter ([standard](http://standardjs.com/))
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:198] - `npm run unit`: runs the unit tests
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:199] - `npm test`: runs both the linter and the tests
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:243] - [Isaac Shlueter](https://github.com/isaacs)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:244] - [Charlie McConnel](https://github.com/avianflu)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:245] - [James Halliday](https://github.com/substack)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/fs-extra/README.md:246] - [Andrew Kelley](https://github.com/andrewrk)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/get-stream/readme.md:83] - When `encoding` is unset, it assumes an [object mode stream](https://nodesource.com/blog/understanding-object-streams/) and collects values emitted from `stream` unmodified. In this case `maxBuffer` refers to the number of items in the array (not the sum of their sizes).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/get-stream/readme.md:85] - When `encoding` is set to `buffer`, it collects an array of buffers. `maxBuffer` refers to the summed byte lengths of every buffer in the array.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/get-stream/readme.md:87] - When `encoding` is set to anything else, it collects an array of strings. `maxBuffer` refers to the summed character lengths of every string in the array.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/get-stream/readme.md:112] - [get-stdin](https://github.com/sindresorhus/get-stdin) - Get stdin as a string or buffer
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/global-agent/node_modules/semver/README.md:414] - `loose`: Be more forgiving about not-quite-valid semver strings.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/global-agent/node_modules/semver/README.md:419] - `includePrerelease`: Set to suppress the [default
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/global-agent/node_modules/semver/README.md:582] - major
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/global-agent/node_modules/semver/README.md:583] - premajor
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/global-agent/node_modules/semver/README.md:584] - minor
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/global-agent/node_modules/semver/README.md:585] - preminor
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/global-agent/node_modules/semver/README.md:586] - patch

- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/global-agent/node_modules/semver/README.md:588] - prerelease

- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:49] - [Plugins](documentation/lets-make-a-plugin.md)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:50] - [Used by 4K+ packages and 1.8M+ repos](https://github.com/sindresorhus/got/network/dependents)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:51] - [Actively maintained](https://github.com/sindresorhus/got/graphs/contributors)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:52] - [Trusted by many companies](#widely-used)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:496] - `lookup` starts when a socket is assigned and ends when the hostname has been resolved. Does not apply when using a Unix domain socket.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:497] - `connect` starts when `lookup` completes (or when the socket is assigned if lookup does not apply to the request) and ends when the socket is connected.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:498] - `secureConnect` starts when `connect` completes and ends when the handshaking process completes (HTTPS only).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:499] - `socket` starts when the socket is connected. See [request.setTimeout](https://nodejs.org/api/http.html#http_request_settimeout_timeout_callback).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:500] - `response` starts when the request has been written to the socket and ends when the response headers are received.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:501] - `send` starts when the socket is connected and ends with the request has been written to the socket.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:502] - `request` starts when the request is initiated and ends when the response's end event fires.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:508] - limit: `2`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:509] - calculateDelay: `({attemptCount, retryOptions, error, computedValue}) => computedValue | Promise<computedValue>`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:510] - methods: `GET` `PUT` `HEAD` `DELETE` `OPTIONS` `TRACE`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:511] - statusCodes: [`408`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/408) [`413`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/413) [`429`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/429) [`500`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/500) [`502`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/502) [`503`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/503) [`504`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/504) [`521`](https://support.cloudflare.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003011431#521error) [`522`](https://support.cloudflare.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003011431#522error) [`524`](https://support.cloudflare.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003011431#524error)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:512] - maxRetryAfter: `undefined`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:513] - errorCodes: `ETIMEDOUT` `ECONNRESET` `EADDRINUSE` `ECONNREFUSED` `EPIPE` `ENOTFOUND` `ENETUNREACH` `EAI_AGAIN`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:527] - `ETIMEDOUT`: One of the [timeout](#timeout) limits were reached.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:528] - `ECONNRESET`: Connection was forcibly closed by a peer.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:529] - `EADDRINUSE`: Could not bind to any free port.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:530] - `ECONNREFUSED`: Connection was refused by the server.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:531] - `EPIPE`: The remote side of the stream being written has been closed.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:532] - `ENOTFOUND`: Couldn't resolve the hostname to an IP address.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:533] - `ENETUNREACH`: No internet connection.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:534] - `EAI_AGAIN`: DNS lookup timed out.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:916] - `response` - The current response object.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:917] - `allItems` - An array of the emitted items.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:918] - `currentItems` - Items from the current response.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1204] - `hostname`: The server hostname (used when connecting)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1205] - `certificate`: The server certificate
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1233] - `options` - The Got options that were set on this request.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1273] - `start` - Time when the request started.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1274] - `socket` - Time when a socket was assigned to the request.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1275] - `lookup` - Time when the DNS lookup finished.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1276] - `connect` - Time when the socket successfully connected.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1277] - `secureConnect` - Time when the socket securely connected.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1278] - `upload` - Time when the request finished uploading.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1279] - `response` - Time when the request fired `response` event.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1280] - `end` - Time when the response fired `end` event.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1281] - `error` - Time when the request fired `error` event.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1282] - `abort` - Time when the request fired `abort` event.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1283] - `phases`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1560] - options are merged using [`got.mergeOptions()`](#gotmergeoptionsparentoptions-newoptions) (including hooks),
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1561] - handlers are stored in an array (you can access them through `instance.defaults.handlers`).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1595] - If the new property is not defined, the old value is used.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1596] - If the new property is explicitly set to `undefined`:
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1599] - If the parent value is an instance of `URLSearchParams`:
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1602] - If the new property is a plain `object`:
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1605] - If the new property is an `Array`, it overwrites the old one with a deep clone of the new property.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1606] - Properties that are not enumerable, such as `context`, `body`, `json`, and `form`, will not be merged.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1607] - Otherwise, the new value is assigned to the key.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1978] - `PROTOCOL` - `http` or `https` *(optional)*
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1979] - `SOCKET` - Absolute path to a unix domain socket, for example: `/var/run/docker.sock`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:1980] - `PATH` - Request path, for example: `/v2/keys`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:2388] - [gh-got](https://github.com/sindresorhus/gh-got) - Got convenience wrapper to interact with the GitHub API
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:2389] - [gl-got](https://github.com/singapore/gl-got) - Got convenience wrapper to interact with the GitLab API
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:2390] - [travis-got](https://github.com/samverschueren/travis-got) - Got convenience wrapper to interact with the Travis API
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:2391] - [graphql-got](https://github.com/kevva/graphql-got) - Got convenience wrapper to interact with GraphQL
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:2392] - [GotQL](https://github.com/khaosdoctor/gotql) - Got convenience wrapper to interact with GraphQL using JSON-parsed queries instead of strings
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:2393] - [got-fetch](https://github.com/alexghr/got-fetch) - Got with a `fetch` interface
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:2403] - [Vsevolod Strukchinsky](https://github.com/floatdrop)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:2404] - [Alexander Tesfamichael](https://github.com/alextes)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/got/readme.md:2405] - [Brandon Smith](https://github.com/brandon93s)
