- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:228] -   `policy` — A new `CachePolicy` with HTTP headers updated from `revalidationResponse`. You can always replace the old cached `CachePolicy` with the new one.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:229] -   `modified` — Boolean indicating whether the response body has changed, and you should use the new response body sent by the server.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:239] -   [ImageOptim API](https://imageoptim.com/api), [make-fetch-happen](https://github.com/zkat/make-fetch-happen), [cacheable-request](https://www.npmjs.com/package/cacheable-request) ([got](https://www.npmjs.com/package/got)), [npm/registry-fetch](https://github.com/npm/registry-fetch), [etc.](https://github.com/kornelski/http-cache-semantics/network/dependents)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:240] -   [Rust version of this library](https://lib.rs/crates/http-cache-semantics).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:244] -   `Cache-Control` response header with all the quirks.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:245] -   `Expires` with check for bad clocks.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:246] -   `Pragma` response header.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:247] -   `Age` response header.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:248] -   `Vary` response header.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:249] -   Default cacheability of statuses and methods.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:250] -   Requests for stale data.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:251] -   Filtering of hop-by-hop headers.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:252] -   Basic revalidation request
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:253] -   `stale-if-error`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:254] -   `stale-while-revalidate`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:258] -   Merging of range requests, `If-Range` (but correctly supports them as non-cacheable)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http-cache-semantics/README.md:259] -   Revalidation of multiple representations
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http2-wrapper/README.md:445] - 32% **less** performant than `http2`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http2-wrapper/README.md:446] - as performant as `https - keepalive`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http2-wrapper/README.md:447] - 100% **more** performant than `http`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http2-wrapper/README.md:450] - 22% **less** performant than `http2`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http2-wrapper/README.md:451] - 8% **more** performant than `https - keepalive`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http2-wrapper/README.md:452] - 118% **more** performant than `http`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http2-wrapper/README.md:455] - 41% **less** performant than `http2`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http2-wrapper/README.md:456] - 8% **less** performant than `https - keepalive`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http2-wrapper/README.md:457] - 89% **more** performant than `http`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http2-wrapper/README.md:460] - 31% **less** performant than `http2`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http2-wrapper/README.md:461] - as performant as `https - keepalive`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/http2-wrapper/README.md:462] - 103% **more** performant than `http`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/json-stringify-safe/CHANGELOG.md:2] - Fixes stringify to only take ancestors into account when checking
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/json-stringify-safe/CHANGELOG.md:7] - Fixes calling the `replacer` function in the proper context (`thisArg`).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/json-stringify-safe/CHANGELOG.md:8] - Fixes calling the `cycleReplacer` function in the proper context (`thisArg`).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/json-stringify-safe/CHANGELOG.md:9] - Speeds serializing by a factor of
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:4] - **BREAKING:** Remove global `spaces` option.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:5] - **BREAKING:** Drop support for Node 0.10, 0.12, and io.js.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:6] - Remove undocumented `passParsingErrors` option.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:7] - Added `EOL` override option to `writeFile` when using `spaces`. [#89]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:12] - Fixed bug in `writeFile` when there was a serialization error & no callback was passed. In previous versions, an empty file would be written; now no file is written.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:17] - Changed behavior of `throws` option for `readFileSync`; now does not throw filesystem errors when `throws` is `false`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:22] - added optional support for `graceful-fs` [#62]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:26] - fix to support BOM. [#45][#45]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:30] - add `throws` to `readFile()`. See [#39][#39]
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:31] - add support for any arbitrary `fs` module. Useful with [mock-fs](https://www.npmjs.com/package/mock-fs)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:35] - include file name in parse error. See: https://github.com/jprichardson/node-jsonfile/pull/34
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:39] - split out tests into separate files
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:40] - fixed `throws` when set to `true` in `readFileSync()`. See: https://github.com/jprichardson/node-jsonfile/pull/33
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:44] - fixed regression when passing in string as encoding for options in `writeFile()` and `writeFileSync()`. See: https://github.com/jprichardson/node-jsonfile/issues/28
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:48] - added `options.spaces` to `writeFile()` and `writeFileSync()`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:52] - fixed if passed `readFileSync(file, 'utf8')`. See: https://github.com/jprichardson/node-jsonfile/issues/25
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:56] - fixed regressions if `null` is passed for options. See: https://github.com/jprichardson/node-jsonfile/issues/24
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:60] - cleanup: JavaScript Standard Style, rename files, dropped terst for assert
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:61] - methods now support JSON revivers/replacers
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/CHANGELOG.md:65] - update license attribute https://github.com/jprichardson/node-jsonfile/pull/21
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/jsonfile/README.md:48] - `throws` (`boolean`, default: `true`). If an error is encountered reading or parsing the file, throw the error. If `false`, returns `null` for the object.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:20] - Isn't bloated
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:21] - Has a simple Promise based API
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:22] - Suitable as a TTL based cache or persistent key-value store
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:23] - [Easily embeddable](#add-cache-support-to-your-module) inside another module
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:24] - Works with any storage that implements the [`Map`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Map) API
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:25] - Handles all JSON types plus `Buffer`
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:26] - Supports namespaces
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:27] - Wide range of [**efficient, well tested**](#official-storage-adapters) storage adapters
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:28] - Connection errors are passed through (db failures won't kill your app)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:29] - Supports the current active LTS version of Node.js or higher
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:146] - [quick-lru](https://github.com/sindresorhus/quick-lru) - Simple "Least Recently Used" (LRU) cache
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:147] - [keyv-file](https://github.com/zaaack/keyv-file) - File system storage adapter for Keyv
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:148] - [keyv-dynamodb](https://www.npmjs.com/package/keyv-dynamodb) - DynamoDB storage adapter for Keyv
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:149] - [keyv-lru](https://www.npmjs.com/package/keyv-lru) - LRU storage adapter for Keyv
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:150] - [keyv-null](https://www.npmjs.com/package/keyv-null) - Null storage adapter for Keyv
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:151] - [keyv-firestore ](https://github.com/goto-bus-stop/keyv-firestore) – Firebase Cloud Firestore adapter for Keyv
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:152] - [keyv-mssql](https://github.com/pmorgan3/keyv-mssql) - Microsoft Sql Server adapter for Keyv
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:153] - [keyv-azuretable](https://github.com/howlowck/keyv-azuretable) - Azure Table Storage/API adapter for Keyv
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:154] - [keyv-arango](https://github.com/TimMikeladze/keyv-arango) - ArangoDB storage adapter for Keyv
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/keyv/README.md:155] - [keyv-momento](https://github.com/momentohq/node-keyv-adaptor/) - Momento storage adapter for Keyv
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/matcher/readme.md:107] - [matcher-cli](https://github.com/sindresorhus/matcher-cli) - CLI for this module
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/matcher/readme.md:108] - [multimatch](https://github.com/sindresorhus/multimatch) - Extends `minimatch.match()` with support for multiple patterns
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/mimic-response/readme.md:48] - [mimic-fn](https://github.com/sindresorhus/mimic-fn) - Make a function mimic another one
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/mimic-response/readme.md:49] - [clone-response](https://github.com/lukechilds/clone-response) - Clone a Node.js response stream
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/ms/readme.md:44] - Works both in [Node.js](https://nodejs.org) and in the browser
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/ms/readme.md:45] - If a number is supplied to `ms`, a string with a unit is returned
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/ms/readme.md:46] - If a string that contains the number is supplied, it returns it as a number (e.g.: it returns `100` for `'100'`)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/ms/readme.md:47] - If you pass a string with a number and a valid unit, the number of equivalent milliseconds is returned
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/ms/readme.md:51] - [ms.macro](https://github.com/knpwrs/ms.macro) - Run `ms` as a macro at build-time.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/normalize-url/readme.md:274] - [compare-urls](https://github.com/sindresorhus/compare-urls) - Compare URLs by first normalizing them
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/p-cancelable/readme.md:151] - [p-progress](https://github.com/sindresorhus/p-progress) - Create a promise that reports progress
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/p-cancelable/readme.md:152] - [p-lazy](https://github.com/sindresorhus/p-lazy) - Create a lazy promise that defers execution until `.then()` or `.catch()` is called
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/p-cancelable/readme.md:153] - [More…](https://github.com/sindresorhus/promise-fun)
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:33] - `curr` current completed index
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:34] - `total` total number of ticks to complete
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:35] - `width` the displayed width of the progress bar defaulting to total
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:36] - `stream` the output stream defaulting to stderr
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:37] - `head` head character defaulting to complete character
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:38] - `complete` completion character defaulting to "="
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:39] - `incomplete` incomplete character defaulting to "-"
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:40] - `renderThrottle` minimum time between updates in milliseconds defaulting to 16
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:41] - `clear` option to clear the bar on completion defaulting to false
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:42] - `callback` optional function to call when the progress bar completes
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:48] - `:bar` the progress bar itself
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:49] - `:current` current tick number
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:50] - `:total` total ticks
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:51] - `:elapsed` time elapsed in seconds
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:52] - `:percent` completion percentage
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:53] - `:eta` estimated completion time in seconds
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/progress/Readme.md:54] - `:rate` rate of ticks per second
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/semver/README.md:311] - `loose`  Be more forgiving about not-quite-valid semver strings.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/semver/README.md:316] - `includePrerelease`  Set to suppress the [default
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:54] - [`Primitive`](source/basic.d.ts) - Matches any [primitive value](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Primitive).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:55] - [`Class`](source/basic.d.ts) - Matches a [`class` constructor](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Classes).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:56] - [`TypedArray`](source/basic.d.ts) - Matches any [typed array](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/TypedArray), like `Uint8Array` or `Float64Array`.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:57] - [`JsonObject`](source/basic.d.ts) - Matches a JSON object.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:58] - [`JsonArray`](source/basic.d.ts) - Matches a JSON array.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:59] - [`JsonValue`](source/basic.d.ts) - Matches any valid JSON value.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:60] - [`ObservableLike`](source/basic.d.ts) - Matches a value that is like an [Observable](https://github.com/tc39/proposal-observable).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:64] - [`Except`](source/except.d.ts) - Create a type from an object type without certain keys. This is a stricter version of [`Omit`](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/release-notes/typescript-3-5.html#the-omit-helper-type).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:65] - [`Mutable`](source/mutable.d.ts) - Convert an object with `readonly` keys into a mutable object. The inverse of `Readonly<T>`.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:66] - [`Merge`](source/merge.d.ts) - Merge two types into a new type. Keys of the second type overrides keys of the first type.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:67] - [`MergeExclusive`](source/merge-exclusive.d.ts) - Create a type that has mutually exclusive keys.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:68] - [`RequireAtLeastOne`](source/require-at-least-one.d.ts) - Create a type that requires at least one of the given keys.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:69] - [`RequireExactlyOne`](source/require-exactly-one.d.ts) - Create a type that requires exactly a single key of the given keys and disallows more.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:70] - [`PartialDeep`](source/partial-deep.d.ts) - Create a deeply optional version of another type. Use [`Partial<T>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1401-L1406) if you only need one level deep.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:71] - [`ReadonlyDeep`](source/readonly-deep.d.ts) - Create a deeply immutable version of an `object`/`Map`/`Set`/`Array` type. Use [`Readonly<T>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1415-L1420) if you only need one level deep.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:72] - [`LiteralUnion`](source/literal-union.d.ts) - Create a union type by combining primitive types and literal types without sacrificing auto-completion in IDEs for the literal type part of the union. Workaround for [Microsoft/TypeScript#29729](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/issues/29729).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:73] - [`Promisable`](source/promisable.d.ts) - Create a type that represents either the value or the value wrapped in `PromiseLike`.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:74] - [`Opaque`](source/opaque.d.ts) - Create an [opaque type](https://codemix.com/opaque-types-in-javascript/).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:75] - [`SetOptional`](source/set-optional.d.ts) - Create a type that makes the given keys optional.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:76] - [`SetRequired`](source/set-required.d.ts) - Create a type that makes the given keys required.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:77] - [`ValueOf`](source/value-of.d.ts) - Create a union of the given object's values, and optionally specify which keys to get the values from.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:78] - [`PromiseValue`](source/promise-value.d.ts) - Returns the type that is wrapped inside a `Promise`.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:79] - [`AsyncReturnType`](source/async-return-type.d.ts) - Unwrap the return type of a function that returns a `Promise`.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:80] - [`ConditionalKeys`](source/conditional-keys.d.ts) - Extract keys from a shape where values extend the given `Condition` type.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:81] - [`ConditionalPick`](source/conditional-pick.d.ts) - Like `Pick` except it selects properties from a shape where the values extend the given `Condition` type.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:82] - [`ConditionalExcept`](source/conditional-except.d.ts) - Like `Omit` except it removes properties from a shape where the values extend the given `Condition` type.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:83] - [`UnionToIntersection`](source/union-to-intersection.d.ts) - Convert a union type to an intersection type.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:84] - [`Stringified`](source/stringified.d.ts) - Create a type with the keys of the given type changed to `string` type.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:88] - [`PackageJson`](source/package-json.d.ts) - Type for [npm's `package.json` file](https://docs.npmjs.com/creating-a-package-json-file).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:89] - [`TsConfigJson`](source/tsconfig-json.d.ts) - Type for [TypeScript's `tsconfig.json` file](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/tsconfig-json.html) (TypeScript 3.7).
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:95] - [`Diff` and `Spread`](https://github.com/sindresorhus/type-fest/pull/7) - The PR author didn't provide any real-world use-cases and the PR went stale. If you think this type is useful, provide some real-world use-cases and we might reconsider.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:96] - [`Dictionary`](https://github.com/sindresorhus/type-fest/issues/33) - You only save a few characters (`Dictionary<number>` vs `Record<string, number>`) from [`Record`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1429-L1434), which is more flexible and well-known. Also, you shouldn't use an object as a dictionary. We have `Map` in JavaScript now.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:97] - [`SubType`](https://github.com/sindresorhus/type-fest/issues/22) - The type is powerful, but lacks good use-cases and is prone to misuse.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:98] - [`ExtractProperties` and `ExtractMethods`](https://github.com/sindresorhus/type-fest/pull/4) - The types violate the single responsibility principle. Instead, refine your types into more granular type hierarchies.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:106] - [`Partial<T>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1401-L1406) - Make all properties in `T` optional.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:153] - [`Required<T>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1408-L1413) - Make all properties in `T` required.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:183] - [`Readonly<T>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1415-L1420) - Make all properties in `T` readonly.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:228] - [`Pick<T, K>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1422-L1427) - From `T`, pick a set of properties whose keys are in the union `K`.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:268] - [`Record<K, T>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1429-L1434) - Construct a type with a set of properties `K` of type `T`.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:311] - [`Exclude<T, U>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1436-L1439) - Exclude from `T` those types that are assignable to `U`.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:345] - [`Extract<T, U>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1441-L1444) - Extract from `T` those types that are assignable to `U`.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:388] - [`NonNullable<T>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1446-L1449) - Exclude `null` and `undefined` from `T`.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:427] - [`Parameters<T>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1451-L1454) - Obtain the parameters of a function type in a tuple.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:455] - [`ConstructorParameters<T>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1456-L1459) - Obtain the parameters of a constructor function type in a tuple.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:503] - [`ReturnType<T>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1461-L1464) – Obtain the return type of a function type.
- [extension/electron-shell/node_modules/type-fest/readme.md:538] - [`InstanceType<T>`](https://github.com/Microsoft/TypeScript/blob/2961bc3fc0ea1117d4e53bc8e97fa76119bc33e3/src/lib/es5.d.ts#L1466-L1469) – Obtain the instance type of a constructor function type.

- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:8] - [Usage](#usage)

- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:10] - [Notes](#notes)
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:14] - Electron is the wrapper UI and process orchestrator.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:15] - Python backend remains the runtime underneath.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:16] - Startup probes a health endpoint first and reuses an already-running backend when available.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:17] - If no backend is running, Electron launches a managed backend process.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:18] - Electron only stops the backend on quit when Electron started that backend instance.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:19] - Startup target can be changed so A.S.S. (or any bootstrap entry) can become whatever backend/app you launch.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:37] - Target script: `launcher/bossforge_launcher.py`
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:38] - Health endpoint: `/api/status`
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:39] - Host: `127.0.0.1`
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:40] - Port: `5005`
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:44] - `BOSSFORGE_APP_NAME`: Window/tray label.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:45] - `BOSSFORGE_PROJECT_ROOT`: Project root used for resolving scripts and cwd.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:46] - `BOSSFORGE_PYTHON`: Explicit Python executable.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:47] - `BOSSFORGE_HOST`: Backend host.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:48] - `BOSSFORGE_PORT`: Backend port.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:49] - `BOSSFORGE_START_SCRIPT`: Relative or absolute Python script to launch.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:50] - `BOSSFORGE_START_ARGS`: Space-separated startup args for custom script.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:51] - `BOSSFORGE_START_ARGS_JSON`: JSON array of startup args (preferred for complex args).
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:52] - `BOSSFORGE_HEALTH_PATH`: Health probe path.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:53] - `BOSSFORGE_APPEND_DEFAULT_ARGS`: Set to `1` to force default `--no-browser --host --port` args for custom script.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:70] - If your custom start script does not accept `--host`/`--port`, leave `BOSSFORGE_APPEND_DEFAULT_ARGS` unset.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:71] - Tray icon path remains `assets/bossforgeos.png`.
- [extension/electron-shell/README.md:72] - You can keep Python as the substrate while swapping launch targets above it.
- [extension/extension_readme.md:11] - Contains the extension workspace integration surface and Electron shell compatibility layer.
- [extension/extension_readme.md:12] - Canonical extension behavior should be tracked in this file to avoid drift between legacy startup docs and active workflows.

- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:7] - [How It Works](#how-it-works)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:8] - [Files](#files)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:9] - [Voice-First Usage](#voice-first-usage)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:10] - [Supported Voice Intents](#supported-voice-intents)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:11] - [Direct Agent Voice Routing](#direct-agent-voice-routing)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:12] - [Next Steps](#next-steps)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:16] - **Observation:** Calls scripts like `system_info.py` and `window_management.py` to gather current state.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:17] - **User Goal:** Prompts for task or goal.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:18] - **Action Decision:** Currently accepts a JSON action from the user (can be replaced with model logic).
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:19] - **Action Execution:** Calls the modular script mapped by the action schema.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:20] - **Loop:** Repeats observation, action, and logging until complete or stopped.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:21] - **Logging:** Stores each step in JSONL episode logs for replay and debugging.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:25] - `command_processor.py`: Main command-processing loop.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:26] - `action_schema.json`: Allowed actions and parameters.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:27] - `observation_format.json`: Example observation structure.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:28] - `audio_dictation.py`: Voice command interpreter and executor.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:29] - `file_lock.py`: Command-code lock state for protected file access.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:30] - `permission_lease.py`: Timed and indefinite action grants.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:34] - Activation moniker is required: `Runeforge` (shorthand `runforge` accepted).
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:35] - Parse spoken text without executing:
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_command_processor.md:37] - Execute voice command text directly:

- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:7] - [File Operation Restrictions](#file-operation-restrictions)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:8] - [Registry Key Protection](#registry-key-protection)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:9] - [Observation Redaction](#observation-redaction)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:10] - [VM and Environment Check](#vm-and-environment-check)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:11] - [High-Risk Action Confirmation](#high-risk-action-confirmation)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:12] - [Extending the Sandbox](#extending-the-sandbox)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:16] - File operations (create, delete, move, copy, rename, list) are restricted to whitelisted directories:
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:20] - Access attempts outside these directories are blocked and logged.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:24] - Access to dangerous registry keys (for example `SYSTEM`, `SAM`, `SECURITY`, Windows startup paths) is blocked.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:25] - Only safe registry operations are allowed.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:29] - Sensitive patterns (passwords, API keys, tokens, secrets, credentials, and related data) are redacted from observations before logging or model input.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:33] - The agent can detect common virtualized environments (VMware, VirtualBox, and others).
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:34] - For maximum safety, run the agent in a VM or similarly restricted environment.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:38] - High-risk actions (file deletion, registry edits, shutdown, and more) require command-code confirmation.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README_sandbox.md:42] - To add restrictions, edit `sandbox.py` and update whitelists or sensitive patterns.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:7] - [Script Categories](#script-categories)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:8] - [Safety](#safety)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:9] - [Usage](#usage)
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:13] - **app_control.py**: Open, close, and list running applications.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:14] - **file_management.py**: Create, delete, move, copy, rename files, and list directories.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:15] - **window_management.py**: Move, resize, minimize, maximize, and close windows.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:16] - **network_tools.py**: Get IP, check connectivity, and list Wi-Fi networks.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:17] - **user_session.py**: Lock, log off, shutdown, restart, and sleep.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:18] - **clipboard_tools.py**: Get and set clipboard text.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:19] - **screenshot_tools.py**: Capture and save screenshots.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:20] - **system_info.py**: Display OS, CPU, RAM, and disk info.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:21] - **process_management.py**: List, kill, and start processes.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:22] - **registry_tools.py**: Read and set Windows registry values.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:23] - **powershell_tools.py**: Run arbitrary PowerShell commands.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:24] - **command_execution.py**: Run arbitrary shell commands.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:25] - **high_risk_action.py**: Utility for requiring command-code confirmation for dangerous actions.
- [Runeforge OS Edition/README.md:26] - **command_code.txt**: Stores your system-wide command code for high-risk actions.

- [README.md:35] - File-based Rune Bus for commands, events, and state snapshots
- [README.md:36] - Modular daemons: Hearth-Tender, SoundForge (legacy SoundStage), Voice, Security, Archivist
- [README.md:37] - BossForge CLI (`bforge`) with status, tail, shell, agent dispatch, ritual record/play/list, plugin support
- [README.md:38] - Control Hall dashboard (Flask/HTML/JS) with panels for agent status, commands, events, sound scheme management, onboarding, scheduling, CI/CD, collaboration, analytics
- [README.md:39] - SoundForge deterministic sound event engine: per-app/event mapping, system sound replacement, rollback, diagnostics, bundle import/export
- [README.md:40] - Deep VS Code extension: onboarding wizard, agent builder, event streaming, import/export, collaborative editing, CLI integration, analytics dashboard
- [README.md:41] - Integrated onboarding, scheduler, CI/CD, and real-time collaboration features
- [README.md:45] - `core/`: bus, daemons, CLI, connectors (GitHub, Hugging Face, SoundForge, Voice)
- [README.md:46] - `modules/`: system modules and snapshots
- [README.md:47] - `ui/`: Control Hall server (Flask/HTML/JS)
- [README.md:48] - `docs/`: architecture, runbook, feature audits, todos, changelogs
- [README.md:49] - `extension/`: VS Code extension (TypeScript/JS)
- [README.md:50] - `tests/`: unit and integration tests
- [README.md:51] - `voices/`: canonical voice-layer profile schema and agent profiles
- [README.md:59] - Deterministic Windows sound event engine (restores true "Open/Close Program" sounds)
- [README.md:60] - Per-app/event mapping, system sound replacement, rollback, diagnostics
- [README.md:61] - Pre-speaker interception, 10-band EQ, stereo-to-7.2 upmix, output switching
- [README.md:62] - PowerShell registry manager for system event sounds with backup/rollback
- [README.md:63] - HTTP API for integration and control
- [README.md:67] - Web-based dashboard (Flask/HTML/JS)
- [README.md:68] - Panels for agent status, commands, events, sound scheme management, onboarding, scheduling, CI/CD, collaboration, analytics

- [README.md:74] - Sidebar panel with onboarding wizard, agent builder, event streaming, import/export, collaborative editing, CLI integration, analytics dashboard
- [README.md:75] - REST API integration with Control Hall and SoundStage
- [README.md:76] - Status bar, command palette, extension settings

- [README.md:159] - Default model path: `.models/runeforge_core-7b`
- [README.md:160] - Default served model name: `runeforge_Core-7b`
- [README.md:161] - Default endpoint: `http://127.0.0.1:8011/v1/chat/completions`
- [README.md:162] - This endpoint is auto-wired to `runeforge_profile.json` (`llm_router`) at startup when available.

- [README.md:179] - If your main BossForge runtime does not have a compatible `vllm` install, bundle a dedicated runtime and pass its interpreter using `--internal-vllm-python`.
- [README.md:180] - Use Python `3.11` or `3.12` for the dedicated runtime bootstrap (`3.14` is not supported).
- [README.md:181] - On Windows, a successful `pip install vllm` can still fail at startup when native extension `vllm._C` is missing. In that case, run vLLM from Linux/WSL and point BossForge to that runtime/interpreter.
- [README.md:182] - WSL run command: `start_bossforge_internal_vllm_wsl.cmd`
- [README.md:183] - Dedicated runtime contract details: `docs/internal_vllm_runtime.md`

- [README.md:413] - Runeforge supports voice-first routing through `Runeforge OS Edition/audio_dictation.py`.
- [README.md:414] - Computer-control voice commands must begin with the Runeforge callout moniker.
- [README.md:417] - Agent-name monikers are supported only for task assignment/delegation.
- [README.md:420] - Direct agent wake commands are enabled. Saying an agent name triggers `status_ping` and a wake response event.
- [README.md:421] - User-defined agent aliases are supported.
- [README.md:422] - Runeforge includes an internal LLM intent router after direct alias/wake matching and before fallback parser rules.
- [README.md:423] - LLM router is suggestion-only. Command processor, command-code, leases, and lock checks remain execution authority.
- [README.md:424] - Continuous voice daemon starts with unified launcher by default.
- [README.md:428] - `Runeforge mute listening`
- [README.md:429] - `Runeforge unmute listening`
- [README.md:430] - `Runeforge stop listening`

- [README.md:435] - `voice_listen` with optional args `{"command_code":"YOUR_CODE"}`
- [README.md:439] - `python -m core.bforge agent runeforge voice_command --args "{\"text\":\"Runeforge lock file \\\"C:/AgentSandbox/Downloads/tool.zip\\\"\",\"command_code\":\"123456\"}"`
